name: Svadilfari
description: Svadilfari (Old Norse: 'unlucky traveler' or 'ill-fated journey') was a legendary stallion in Norse mythology, renowned for his supernatural strength and speed. He belonged to an unnamed giant builder who offered to construct Asgard's walls. Svadilfari hauled stones at an impossibly fast rate, nearly winning the wager for his master. When Loki transformed into a mare to lure Svadilfari away and prevent the giant's victory, their union produced Sleipnir, Odin's eight-legged steed. Svadilfari represents the magical nature of horses in Norse tradition and their connection to the otherworld.
physicalDescription: A magnificent gray stallion of supernatural size and strength, capable of hauling massive boulders at incredible speeds. His coat gleamed like polished iron, and his hooves struck sparks from stone.

keyMyths.title: The Building of Asgard's Wall
keyMyths.description: A giant builder wagered that he could construct impenetrable walls around Asgard within one winter, demanding the sun, moon, and Freyja as payment. The gods agreed, believing it impossible. However, Svadilfari's supernatural strength allowed his master to haul stones at an alarming rate, nearly completing the task.

keyMyths.title: Loki's Deception and Sleipnir's Birth
keyMyths.description: With three days remaining and the wall nearly complete, the gods demanded Loki fix the situation. Loki transformed into a beautiful mare and lured Svadilfari away into the forest for many days. Their union produced Sleipnir, the eight-legged horse that became Odin's steed.

sources.title: Prose Edda by Snorri Sturluson (Gylfaginning, chapter 42)
Simek, Rudolf - Dictionary of Northern Mythology
Lindow, John - Norse Mythology: A Guide to the Gods, Heroes, Rituals, and Beliefs
